findings.outcome: Supabase JWT authentication no longer promotes arbitrary authenticated users to admin.
Subscription enforcement is fail-closed with bounded explicit test policy.
The public shared application credential and backend legacy-key fallback were removed.
Scheduler routes and callers use the dedicated canonical scheduler-secret boundary.
Mutation-capable scheduler, pipeline and internal routes use explicit authorization middleware.
Automatic FORCE_BOOT_TEST startup database writes were removed.
Public direct-insight reads use the anon key; privileged pipeline writes use the server-only service-role key. Production configuration, rotation and revocation proof were not executed.
RLS policy design and migration execution remain a separately authorized mini-project.

nextAction: ESEC-001-I1 source-and-test remediation PASS. AUTH-001, SUB-001, KEY-001, CRON-001, ROUTE-001 and DB-001 are source-closed and tested; CRED-001 source credential separation is closed but production configuration and rotation proof remain unverified. RLS-001 remains OPEN and separately excluded. Keep ESEC-001 PARTIAL and all runtime gates BLOCKED. Next separately authorize RLS-001 or production runtime/deployment verification; do not deploy, execute E2E proof, activate Scout transport, apply RLS migrations or clear any gate without explicit approval.

failureClasses: Pre-existing asset-register enum, count and unregistered-path drift
